message:
perf: add will_wake guards to eliminate redundant waker clones across async subsystems

Add Waker::will_wake() checks before cloning wakers in poll
implementations throughout the codebase. When an executor re-polls a
future with the same waker (the common case), this avoids an unnecessary
Arc clone + drop cycle per poll. On ARM targets this also eliminates
a store-release barrier per waker update.

Affected futures and async primitives:
- JoinHandle (runtime/builder.rs): guard waker clone in poll
- BlockingOneshotReceiver (runtime/spawn_blocking.rs): guard in poll
- DeliveryReceiver (messaging/kafka.rs): guard in poll
- TcpStreamInner (net/tcp/split.rs): guard both read_waker and
  write_waker paths
- VirtualTcpStream (net/tcp/virtual_tcp.rs): guard read/write half
  wakers; also copy directly from VecDeque slices to unfilled buf
  instead of collecting into intermediate Vec
- VirtualTcpListener (net/tcp/virtual_tcp.rs): guard accept wakers
  in both poll_accept paths
- UnixStreamInner (net/unix/split.rs): mirror TCP split will_wake
  guards for read/write wakers
- Sleep (time/sleep.rs): guard state.waker clone in poll

Transport layer waker management:
- Delay (transport/mock.rs): cache last_waker in Mutex to skip
  UpdateWaker channel sends when waker unchanged; add
  upsert_sim_waiter helper to deduplicate SimWaiter registration
  logic for both send and recv waker vectors
- ChannelSink (transport/sink.rs): add upsert_channel_waiter helper
  extracting the find-or-push pattern; use will_wake guard in
  existing-waiter branch

Also add VecDeque::with_capacity and VecDeque::reserve for virtual
TCP channel buffers, accept queues, and named constants for default
capacities (VIRTUAL_TCP_CHANNEL_CAPACITY_BYTES,
VIRTUAL_TCP_ACCEPT_QUEUE_CAPACITY).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
